seoTitle: Tea Tree vs Temma — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Tea Tree and Temma. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Tea Tree vs Temma: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Tea Tree and Temma.
heading: Tea Tree vs Temma — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Tea Tree, Tasmania and Temma, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Temma has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Tea Tree (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Tea Tree or Temma safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Tea Tree has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Temma scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Tea Tree scores 48/100 for bushfire risk. Temma scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Tea Tree or Temma?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Temma has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.33% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
